title: Cynanchum gilbertii
taxonomy: Cynanchum gilbertii is classified within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies. The genus Cynanchum is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Americas, and Cynanchum gilbertii is one of the species found in this group. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, comprising over 5,000 species. Cynanchum gilbertii's classification is an example of how botanical classification is used to understand the relationships between different plant species.
uses: Cynanchum gilbertii, a plant native to China, has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits.
